title: critoniopsis zamorensis
intro: Critoniopsis zamorensis, also known as Zamorano or Mountain Gold, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. Critoniopsis zamorensis has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, burns, and respiratory issues, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of Critoniopsis zamorensis include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or mixing its essential oils with a carrier oil.
